title: Nervilia juliana
taxonomy: Nervilia juliana belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is a species of orchid. It is classified as a terrestrial orchid and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The genus Nervilia is a group of orchids characterized by their unique leaf structure and slender growth habit. Nervilia juliana is a member of this genus, which includes several other species of orchids. The species Nervilia juliana is also classified as Nervilia juliana in the botanical nomenclature.

intro: Nervilia juliana, also known as the "Julian's Yellow Fragrant Orchid," is a species of orchid that belongs to the Nervilia genus. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Nervilia juliana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Nervilia juliana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often marketed for their potential to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and reduce stress.
